assessments.rationale: IBC Chapter 10 and NFPA 101 impose the same single-motion egress requirement as EN 179/EN 1125. The product is certified to these standards and adds zero operational steps. Threshold never approached.

NFPA 101 mandates power-independent egress hardware — identical to EltVTR. Product's mechanical-primary architecture pre-satisfies this absolute constraint in all building markets. IoT module is supervisory only and does not affect alarm function.

Typical K-12 school footprint (5,500-9,000 m2, 1-3 stories) places some perimeter exits 40-80 m from a central gateway. Avg. building age 49 years means concrete construction attenuates 868 MHz. One or two repeaters resolve coverage at low cost.
assessments.mitigation: Deploy 1-2 wireless repeaters per building to extend coverage to 60-90 m; Conduct RF site survey before installation to map attenuation from concrete construction; Position central gateway at main corridor junction for maximum line-of-sight coverage

assessments.rationale: Emergency exits (stairwells, perimeter) have lower event frequency than main corridor doors. School facilities teams already run annual NFPA 25 inspections; battery check integrates into existing workflow at zero incremental cost. Battery-monitoring variant further reduces risk.
assessments.mitigation: Add battery check to existing annual/semi-annual safety inspection PM schedule; Specify battery-monitoring variant at procurement for low-battery dashboard alerts; Prioritize highest-event-frequency exits for proactive battery swap scheduling

assessments.rationale: K-12 schools are institutional buildings with utilitarian door hardware. Surface-mount handle guards are standard and uncontroversial. No premium architectural constraints. Glass-frame mounting plate (Art. 991170) handles minority glass-door cases.

L5.d.2 exit alarm systems are rarely integrated with building BMS in K-12 today — fire alarm, access control, and HVAC run as separate systems in most schools. Protocol lock-in is a concern only for districts building consolidated safety platforms. GfS BUS gateway bridges the gap where needed.
assessments.mitigation: Deploy GfS wireless BUS gateway for BACnet/IP translation at districts requiring BMS integration; Position as standalone safety dashboard for the majority of districts (no BMS integration required); Roadmap API/MQTT bridge for tech-forward districts consolidating onto unified safety platforms

assessments.rationale: Per-building cost ($8K-$16K for 10-20 exits) is within scope of federal and state school safety grants. Bipartisan Safer Communities Act 2022 authorized $300M specifically for school security hardware. Budget constraint is a procurement problem, not a market disqualifier.
assessments.mitigation: Position against STOP School Violence Act grants and state post-Uvalde safety funding; Develop ROI case: one security post replacement (~$40K/year) pays back 10+ doors in year 1; Negotiate volume pricing for district-wide multi-school deployments

assessments.rationale: US: FERPA applicability minimal — anonymous door-event logs (no student identity linkage) are facility records, not education records. EU: standard GDPR workflow (DPA, DPIA for systematic monitoring) applies. Both paths are well-established and low-effort.
assessments.mitigation: Configure 90-day rolling data retention cap aligned with standard school security policies; Confirm event logs contain door ID + timestamp only (no identity linkage at emergency exits); Execute DPA with cloud monitoring SaaS provider for EU school deployments

assessments.rationale: K-12 school environments present no IP challenges beyond standard weather exposure at perimeter exits (covered by IP45 outdoor variant). No washdown environments, no chemical processing, no marine conditions at emergency exits. Threshold not approached.

Applies only when combined with powered locking components (electronic classroom locks, electromagnetic holders). Most K-12 emergency exit deployments use the Türwächter as a standalone alarm/monitor — no combined locking required. System-design review if combined configuration specified.
assessments.mitigation: Specify fail-safe (power-off = release) mode on any co-installed electromagnetic holder; Document combined-system compliance in AHJ project submittal for US school projects; Scope Türwächter as standalone monitoring (no combined powered locking) to avoid EN 13637 review for standard installations
